steps.title: Identify what the blank must do
steps.content: The sentence says that being frequently cited ______ the usefulness of Kaminsky's research. The dash then explains: other economists find her studies valuable. The blank must describe how frequent citations relate to that usefulness.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Substitute each choice into the sentence:
  • Choice A — 'belies': means contradicts — frequent citations would be contradicting usefulness, the opposite of what the context supports. ✗
  • Choice B — 'forestalls': means prevents or delays — citations cannot prevent usefulness; this reverses the meaning. ✗
  • Choice C — 'underscores': means emphasizes or highlights — frequent citations by peers highlight how useful the research is, which matches the explanation after the dash. ✓
  • Choice D — 'overshadows': means obscures or eclipses — this would mean citations diminish the usefulness, contradicting the context. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
